text:
It's a bright and cheery Saturday morning. I'm sitting outside my favorite café sipping freshly brewed coffee. Across town, a squirrel tosses a nut aside as it clacks its teeth on a twig. I laugh. Who doesn't love those little critters?

A couple walking their dog stop near me. I greet them enthusiastically. They're on their way to visit my Aunt Lucy who's recovering from surgery. "Oh!" she'd say anytime I mentioned squirrels. I can't help but admire her persistence.

I start talking about my latest literary pursuit. It's an old classic. "You might enjoy 'Anna Karenina,' wouldn't you say?" I wonder aloud. The couple share amused glances. I continue about Tolstoy's characters.

As we chat, a bird swoops down, catching a caterpillar. I can't resist telling them that reminds me of Aunt Lucy catching bugs. "Oh my," says the woman. "She's got an impressive collection
